stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In 1903, African American inventor Mary Kenner invented an improved sanitary belt.
  • It improved on the existing design of personal hygiene products.
  • It made daily life more convenient for many women.
  • In 1923, African American inventor Garrett Morgan invented a three-position traffic signal.
  • It improved on the existing design of two-position traffic signals.
  • It made urban intersections safer for drivers and pedestrians.
stem: The student wants to emphasize a similarity between Kenner's invention and Morgan's invention. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Kenner's improved sanitary belt and Morgan's three-position traffic signal both improved on existing designs.
In 1903, Kenner invented an improved sanitary belt, which made daily life more convenient for many women.
Morgan's three-position traffic signal, which he invented in 1923, made urban intersections safer for drivers and pedestrians.
Kenner's invention made daily life more convenient for many women, whereas Morgan's invention made urban intersections safer.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. It compares Kenner's and Morgan's inventions to one another and emphasizes what they have in common.

Choice B is incorrect. This choice doesn't emphasize a similarity. It only mentions Kenner's invention. It doesn't compare it to Morgan's invention. Choice C is incorrect. This choice doesn't emphasize a similarity. It only mentions Morgan's invention. It doesn't compare it to Kenner's invention. Choice D is incorrect. This choice doesn't emphasize a similarity between the two inventions. Instead, it emphasizes a difference.
